core_mechanism: an age-since-event structured population model (renewal / McKendrick-von Foerster type) in which each unit carries an internal 'age' clock that weights its contribution to a shared accumulating stimulus, and a unit's probability of switching state is a monotone dose-response function of the total accumulated stimulus it has received
brief_reason: The abstract builds a discrete age-of-infection structure that implicitly sets each infectious unit's load (viral load) and makes the probability of a state transition (developing infection) an explicit function of the accumulated load from contacts, which is a dose-response transition on an age-structured renewal process.
